evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Defining data modelling, testing, and documentation standards; Improving data quality through automated testing and validation
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Testing standards, automated validation, and documentation dominate the responsibilities with no velocity language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Designing, building, and maintaining scalable data models using dbt
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Errors affect internal analytics and business decisions without stated regulatory or financial-reporting consequence.
evidence.data_team_maturity: owning the transformation layer of a modern cloud data platform
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A modern cloud platform already exists with named tooling (dbt, Snowflake, Airflow) implying a growing, mid-maturity function within a small team.
evidence.jd_authorship: Developing analytics-ready transformation layers in Snowflake; Orchestrating workflows using Airflow
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise named tools and specific practices (dimensional models, ELT architectures) show exactly what the role involves.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: owning the transformation layer of a modern cloud data platform
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is the internal data function itself — the transformation layer and analytics engineering practice.
evidence.autonomy_level: real ownership over the analytics engineering function; Defining data modelling, testing, and documentation standards
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Owning the transformation layer and defining standards for the wider team are unambiguously strategic verbs.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools or AI-consuming infrastructure anywhere in the posting.
evidence.testing_framing: Defining data modelling, testing, and documentation standards
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing standards are framed as something the candidate defines and owns, not just a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Improving data quality through automated testing and validation
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: data quality and testing are emphasized but there is no compliance, audit, or regulatory framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Building reliable ELT pipelines using SQL and Python
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of building new transformation-layer capability and maintaining/improving existing modern cloud platform.
evidence.work_arrangement: Dublin, County Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid - 1 day per week)
